mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes or OpenShift with ease. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kafka clusters, making it suitable for both development and production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Kafka operator.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts/

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Strimzi Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator using Helm, which will manage your Kafka clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka-namespace

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka-namespace --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Kafka operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Create a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD) that the Strimzi operator manages.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/0.44.0/examples/kafka/kafka-persistent.yaml

```

> Deploys a Kafka cluster with persistent storage.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Kafka cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace

kubectl get pods -n kafka-namespace

```

> Lists Kafka clusters in the specified namespace.
> Verifies that all Kafka pods are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Kafka cluster and all its data.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka-namespace

kubectl delete namespace kafka-namespace

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Kafka data. This step will upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka-namespace --version 0.44.0

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace

```

> Backs up the current Kafka cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Kafka cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Kafka cluster is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the Kafka pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Kafka custom resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in CrashLoopBackOff state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Configuration errors in the Kafka custom resource.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kafka-namespace' and correct the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to Kafka broker
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct network policies are in place and that the Kafka service is properly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Kafka topic creation fails
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions for the user trying to create the topic.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the user has the necessary permissions in the Kafka ACLs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of the 'kafka.spec.kafka.version' field in favor of 'kafka.spec.kafka.image'.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your Kafka custom resources to use the new image field.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with its status as 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
